meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Finish Apps
content.seo_description: Overcome project paralysis with the Antigravity template. A beginner-friendly guide to shipping your first AI app using Claude Code and expert workflows.
content.answer_block: The 'Antigravity' template solves project paralysis by enforcing a modular, feature-gated architecture. By using Claude Code to scaffold the MVP, you bypass the 'blank page' syndrome. This structured approach forces you to build in 48-hour sprints, ensuring your portfolio grows with shippable, production-ready code rather than abandoned repositories.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the weight of a thousand 'beginner project' tutorials pressing down on you. You've started a dozen apps, only to lose momentum the moment you hit a complex state management bug. The graveyard of half-finished repositories is growing, and with it, your imposter syndrome. 

But the gap between a student and a developer isn't intelligence—it's execution. The Antigravity template is designed to collapse that distance by providing a rigid, high-velocity skeleton. You stop worrying about architecture and start focusing on shipping features that actually work.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Core Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project using the Antigravity standard to ensure consistent folder structure and environment configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity template with Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase integration.

steps.title: Define the Feature Schema
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your database schema before writing a single line of frontend code to prevent logic drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a task management app with user authentication and real-time updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create prisma/schema.prisma based on the provided feature requirements'

steps.title: Implement the MVP Component
steps.description: Focus on the core functionality. Don't style yet; build the engine that powers your app's main value prop.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build the core data fetching hook for the dashboard using React Query and the Antigravity data layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the main dashboard logic using the existing Antigravity service layer'

steps.title: Inject Error Boundaries
steps.description: Antigravity relies on graceful degradation. Add error boundaries early to ensure your app doesn't crash during user testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Wrap the main application entry point in an ErrorBoundary and add logging for failed API calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add comprehensive error handling and UI fallback components to the layout.tsx'

steps.title: Automated Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your repo to Vercel or your preferred provider to get a live URL immediately, validating your progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup the CI/CD pipeline configuration file for Vercel deployment'

pro_tips: Always commit your work after every single 'Claude Code' success to maintain a clean git history for your portfolio.
Use the '10-minute rule': If you spend more than 10 minutes debugging a UI quirk, ask Claude Code to refactor the component entirely.
Treat the Antigravity template as a contract; do not modify the core folder structure until you have shipped your first version.
Focus on 'feature flags' to hide incomplete work so you can deploy to production even when the app is only 50% finished.
Keep your README.md updated via Claude Code commands every time you finish a milestone to automatically generate your project documentation.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, share the link in our community showcase channel to receive constructive feedback from senior engineers.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming about your portfolio and start shipping real products that recruiters actually notice. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
